title: Auxiliary Trait Selection with Coheterogeneity
text: vignette describes first step IBMR workflow: selecting auxiliary outcome trait given exposure primary outcome. central motivation closely related outcomes may share overlapping valid instruments, may reflect similar mechanisms instrument invalidity, respect common exposure. shared structure can identified, auxiliary trait may used improve downstream MR estimation outlier detection. IBMR operationalizes idea coheterogeneity_Q(), computes pairwise coheterogeneity outcome traits using SNP-level summary statistics.

title: Auxiliary Trait Selection with Coheterogeneity
text: minimum, coheterogeneity analysis requires: BetaXG: SNP-exposure associations seBetaXG: standard errors BetaXG BetaYG_matrix: matrix SNP-outcome associations seBetaYG_matrix: matrix standard errors corresponding BetaYG_matrix row corresponds SNP, rows must aligned across vectors matrices. common screening setting, columns BetaYG_matrix : one primary outcome one candidate auxiliary outcomes

title: Auxiliary Trait Selection with Coheterogeneity
text: package includes toy dataset called toy_ibmr_example. object contains: one exposure vector one primary outcome two candidate auxiliary traits small formatted data frame IBPRESSO() metadata describing simulation setup simulation deliberately simple. constructed aux_trait_1 shares latent structure primary outcome aux_trait_2, making natural auxiliary trait example.
code: data("toy_ibmr_example")  names(toy_ibmr_example) #> [1] "BetaXG"                "seBetaXG"              "BetaYG_matrix"         #> [4] "seBetaYG_matrix"       "primary_trait"         "candidate_auxiliaries" #> [7] "recommended_auxiliary" "dat_ibpresso_aux1"     "simulation_setup" toy_ibmr_example$primary_trait #> [1] "primary_trait" toy_ibmr_example$candidate_auxiliaries #> [1] "aux_trait_1" "aux_trait_2" str(toy_ibmr_example$simulation_setup) #> List of 11 #>  $ seed                        : num 123 #>  $ n_snps                      : num 80 #>  $ exposure_mean               : num 0.08 #>  $ exposure_sd                 : num 0.02 #>  $ exposure_se                 : num 0.01 #>  $ outcome_se                  : num 0.02 #>  $ primary_effect_on_exposure  : num 0.2 #>  $ aux1_effect_on_exposure     : num 0.25 #>  $ aux1_shared_component_weight: num 0.8 #>  $ aux2_effect_on_exposure     : num -0.05 #>  $ shared_component_sd         : num 0.015

title: Auxiliary Trait Selection with Coheterogeneity
text: begin extracting summary-statistics objects. now compute coheterogeneity across primary outcome candidate auxiliary traits. principal outputs : rho: pairwise coheterogeneity estimates p_value: pairwise significance levels flag: diagnostic status pair K: number SNPs contributing pairwise calculation
code: BetaXG <- toy_ibmr_example$BetaXG seBetaXG <- toy_ibmr_example$seBetaXG BetaYG_matrix <- toy_ibmr_example$BetaYG_matrix seBetaYG_matrix <- toy_ibmr_example$seBetaYG_matrix  colnames(BetaYG_matrix) #> [1] "primary_trait" "aux_trait_1"   "aux_trait_2" cohet_res <- coheterogeneity_Q(   BetaXG = BetaXG,   BetaYG_matrix = BetaYG_matrix,   seBetaXG = seBetaXG,   seBetaYG_matrix = seBetaYG_matrix,   F_min = 5,   min_K_pair = 20 ) round(cohet_res$rho, 3) #>               primary_trait aux_trait_1 aux_trait_2 #> primary_trait             1          NA          NA #> aux_trait_1              NA           1          NA #> aux_trait_2              NA          NA           1 round(cohet_res$p_value, 3) #>               primary_trait aux_trait_1 aux_trait_2 #> primary_trait             0          NA          NA #> aux_trait_1              NA           0          NA #> aux_trait_2              NA          NA           0 cohet_res$flag #>               primary_trait aux_trait_1 aux_trait_2 #> primary_trait "diag"        "tau0"      "tau0"      #> aux_trait_1   "tau0"        "diag"      "tau0"      #> aux_trait_2   "tau0"        "tau0"      "diag" cohet_res$K #>               primary_trait aux_trait_1 aux_trait_2 #> primary_trait             0          80          80 #> aux_trait_1              80           0          80 #> aux_trait_2              80          80           0

title: Auxiliary Trait Selection with Coheterogeneity
text: diagonal entries rho equal 1 definition. quantities primary interest -diagonal entries involving primary outcome. toy example, aux_trait_1 usually exhibit stronger coheterogeneity primary outcome aux_trait_2. practical interpretation framework : larger abs(rho) indicates stronger shared heterogeneity pattern smaller p_value provides stronger evidence coheterogeneity flag checked ensure pairwise estimate usable important diagnostic flags : "OK": pairwise comparison successfully estimated "tau0": corrected heterogeneity estimate identifiable "se NA": coheterogeneity estimate computed, standard error reliably estimated
code: primary_name <- toy_ibmr_example$primary_trait cohet_res$rho[primary_name, ] #> primary_trait   aux_trait_1   aux_trait_2  #>             1            NA            NA cohet_res$p_value[primary_name, ] #> primary_trait   aux_trait_1   aux_trait_2  #>             0            NA            NA cohet_res$flag[primary_name, ] #> primary_trait   aux_trait_1   aux_trait_2  #>        "diag"        "tau0"        "tau0"

title: Auxiliary Trait Selection with Coheterogeneity
text: screening step usually carried ranking candidate auxiliaries primary outcome. practical selection rule : discard pairs unusable flags prioritize pairs finite informative p_value among remaining candidates, select trait largest abs(rho) toy dataset, intended selected auxiliary trait :
code: ranking <- data.frame(   aux_trait = colnames(cohet_res$rho),   rho = cohet_res$rho[primary_name, ],   p_value = cohet_res$p_value[primary_name, ],   flag = cohet_res$flag[primary_name, ],   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  ranking <- subset(ranking, aux_trait != primary_name) ranking$abs_rho <- abs(ranking$rho) ranking <- ranking[order(-ranking$abs_rho), ] ranking #>               aux_trait rho p_value flag abs_rho #> aux_trait_1 aux_trait_1  NA      NA tau0      NA #> aux_trait_2 aux_trait_2  NA      NA tau0      NA toy_ibmr_example$recommended_auxiliary #> [1] "aux_trait_1"

title: Auxiliary Trait Selection with Coheterogeneity
text: coheterogeneity_Q() includes several guardrails stabilize estimation: optional SNP restriction SNP_keep weak-instrument filtering F_min small-exposure filtering bx_min optional winsorization winsor_theta pair-level diagnostic flags especially useful large-scale screening settings traits may sparse unstable SNP-level signal.

title: Auxiliary Trait Selection with Coheterogeneity
text: auxiliary trait selected, can carried downstream instrument-borrowing analysis using: IBMODE() multidimensional mode-based estimation IBPRESSO() outlier detection correction auxiliary trait end--end downstream workflow illustrated companion vignette: instrument-borrowing-workflow

title: Instrument Borrowing Workflow
text: vignette illustrates full IBMR workflow: load SNP-level summary statistics screen candidate auxiliary traits using coheterogeneity select informative auxiliary trait use selected auxiliary trait IBMODE() use auxiliary trait IBPRESSO() purpose demonstrate auxiliary-trait selection downstream instrument borrowing fit together single analysis pipeline.

title: Instrument Borrowing Workflow
text: toy example contains one primary outcome two candidate auxiliary outcomes. data simulated aux_trait_1 usually informative auxiliary trait primary outcome.
code: data("toy_ibmr_example")  BetaXG <- toy_ibmr_example$BetaXG seBetaXG <- toy_ibmr_example$seBetaXG BetaYG_matrix <- toy_ibmr_example$BetaYG_matrix seBetaYG_matrix <- toy_ibmr_example$seBetaYG_matrix primary_name <- toy_ibmr_example$primary_trait candidate_aux <- toy_ibmr_example$candidate_auxiliaries

what: Step 1: Screen Auxiliary Traits with Coheterogeneity
title: Instrument Borrowing Workflow
text: focus row corresponding primary outcome.
code: cohet_res <- coheterogeneity_Q(   BetaXG = BetaXG,   BetaYG_matrix = BetaYG_matrix,   seBetaXG = seBetaXG,   seBetaYG_matrix = seBetaYG_matrix,   F_min = 5,   min_K_pair = 20 )  round(cohet_res$rho, 3) #>               primary_trait aux_trait_1 aux_trait_2 #> primary_trait             1          NA          NA #> aux_trait_1              NA           1          NA #> aux_trait_2              NA          NA           1 round(cohet_res$p_value, 3) #>               primary_trait aux_trait_1 aux_trait_2 #> primary_trait             0          NA          NA #> aux_trait_1              NA           0          NA #> aux_trait_2              NA          NA           0 cohet_res$flag #>               primary_trait aux_trait_1 aux_trait_2 #> primary_trait "diag"        "tau0"      "tau0"      #> aux_trait_1   "tau0"        "diag"      "tau0"      #> aux_trait_2   "tau0"        "tau0"      "diag" rho_primary <- cohet_res$rho[primary_name, ] p_primary <- cohet_res$p_value[primary_name, ] flag_primary <- cohet_res$flag[primary_name, ]  rho_primary #> primary_trait   aux_trait_1   aux_trait_2  #>             1            NA            NA p_primary #> primary_trait   aux_trait_1   aux_trait_2  #>             0            NA            NA flag_primary #> primary_trait   aux_trait_1   aux_trait_2  #>        "diag"        "tau0"        "tau0"

title: Instrument Borrowing Workflow
text: selected auxiliary trait can taken candidate largest absolute coheterogeneity among acceptable diagnostic flags. packaged toy example, intended selected auxiliary trait :
code: ranking <- data.frame(   aux_trait = candidate_aux,   rho = rho_primary[candidate_aux],   p_value = p_primary[candidate_aux],   flag = flag_primary[candidate_aux],   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  ranking$abs_rho <- abs(ranking$rho) ranking <- ranking[order(-ranking$abs_rho), ] ranking #>               aux_trait rho p_value flag abs_rho #> aux_trait_1 aux_trait_1  NA      NA tau0      NA #> aux_trait_2 aux_trait_2  NA      NA tau0      NA chosen_aux <- ranking$aux_trait[1] chosen_aux #> [1] "aux_trait_1" toy_ibmr_example$recommended_auxiliary #> [1] "aux_trait_1"

title: Instrument Borrowing Workflow
text: auxiliary trait selected, subset outcome matrices primary outcome chosen auxiliary trait. run IBMODE(). returns joint mode-based estimates primary auxiliary outcomes. routine analyses, number bootstrap replicates chosen balance computational cost stability.
code: BetaYG_mode <- BetaYG_matrix[, c(primary_name, chosen_aux), drop = FALSE] seBetaYG_mode <- seBetaYG_matrix[, c(primary_name, chosen_aux), drop = FALSE] ibmode_res <- IBMODE(   BetaXG = BetaXG,   BetaYG_matrix = BetaYG_mode,   seBetaXG = seBetaXG,   seBetaYG_matrix = seBetaYG_mode,   phi = c(1, 0.5),   n_boot = 200 )  ibmode_res

title: Instrument Borrowing Workflow
text: IBPRESSO() expects data frame rather matrices. toy dataset includes ready-made example recommended auxiliary trait. analysis can run follows. analysis uses selected auxiliary trait improve outlier detection joint primary-auxiliary residual space.
code: dat_ibpresso <- toy_ibmr_example$dat_ibpresso_aux1 head(dat_ibpresso) #>   beta_exposure se_exposure beta_primary se_primary    beta_aux se_aux #> 1    0.06879049        0.01  0.024371675       0.02 0.009380572   0.02 #> 2    0.07539645        0.01  0.010366726       0.02 0.018450490   0.02 #> 3    0.11117417        0.01  0.004073380       0.02 0.038306228   0.02 #> 4    0.08141017        0.01  0.058358081       0.02 0.016712024   0.02 #> 5    0.08258575        0.01  0.009041277       0.02 0.016210084   0.02 #> 6    0.11430130        0.01  0.030819265       0.02 0.051580327   0.02 ibpresso_res <- IBPRESSO(   BetaOutcome = "beta_primary",   BetaExposure = "beta_exposure",   BetaAux = "beta_aux",   SdOutcome = "se_primary",   SdExposure = "se_exposure",   SdAux = "se_aux",   data = dat_ibpresso,   OUTLIERtest = TRUE,   DISTORTIONtest = FALSE,   NbDistribution = 200,   seed = 123 )  ibpresso_res

title: Instrument Borrowing Workflow
text: key logic instrument-borrowing approach : identify related auxiliary trait meaningful coheterogeneity preserve primary outcome principal target inference use auxiliary trait stabilize downstream robust MR analysis auxiliary trait therefore alternative endpoint. Instead, informative companion trait used strengthen inference primary outcome.

title: Instrument Borrowing Workflow
text: Ensure SNP order aligned across inputs. Harmonize alleles constructing summary-statistics matrices. Examine flag K coheterogeneity_Q() selecting auxiliary trait. Use one candidate auxiliary trait whenever scientifically reasonable, since screening useful several plausible auxiliaries available. Increase n_boot IBMODE() NbDistribution IBPRESSO() final analyses.

title: Instrument Borrowing Workflow
text: IBMR supports two-stage strategy: first, identify suitable auxiliary trait using coheterogeneity second, use auxiliary trait improve robust MR estimation outlier detection framework particularly useful related outcome traits expected share informative instrument-validity structure relative common exposure.

code: @Manual{,   title = {IBMR: Instrument Borrowing for Mendelian Randomization},   author = {Anagh Chattopadhyay},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/achatto4/IBMR}, }

title: IBMR
text: IBMR R package instrument borrowing Mendelian randomization (MR) using summary-level genetic association data. package motivated setting primary outcome may share overlapping valid instruments, similar mechanisms instrument invalidity, one related auxiliary outcomes given exposure. shared structure present, auxiliary traits can used improve robustness efficiency downstream MR analysis. IBMR provides three main components: coheterogeneity_Q() coheterogeneity-based auxiliary trait screening IBMODE() multidimensional mode-based estimation instrument borrowing IBPRESSO() MR-PRESSO auxiliary trait

title: IBMR
text: website organized around workflow implemented package: provide SNP-level summary statistics exposure, primary outcome, one candidate auxiliary outcomes screen candidate auxiliary traits using coheterogeneity select informative auxiliary trait carry selected auxiliary trait downstream robust MR analysis

title: IBMR
text: Install package GitHub :

title: IBMR
code: library(IBMR) data("toy_ibmr_example")  cohet_res <- coheterogeneity_Q(   BetaXG = toy_ibmr_example$BetaXG,   BetaYG_matrix = toy_ibmr_example$BetaYG_matrix,   seBetaXG = toy_ibmr_example$seBetaXG,   seBetaYG_matrix = toy_ibmr_example$seBetaYG_matrix,   F_min = 5,   min_K_pair = 20 )  round(cohet_res$rho, 3) cohet_res$flag

what: Multidimensional Mode-Based Estimation for Mendelian Randomization — IBMODE
title: Multidimensional Mode-Based Estimation for Mendelian Randomization — IBMODE
text: Performs mode-based estimation (MBE) multiple correlated outcomes simultaneously using multidimensional kernel density estimation. approach identifies modal causal effect across multiple traits accounting pleiotropy.

title: Multidimensional Mode-Based Estimation for Mendelian Randomization — IBMODE
code: IBMODE(   BetaXG,   BetaYG_matrix,   seBetaXG,   seBetaYG_matrix,   phi = c(1, 0.5, 0.25),   n_boot = 10000,   alpha = 0.05 )

title: Multidimensional Mode-Based Estimation for Mendelian Randomization — IBMODE
text: BetaXG Numeric vector SNP-exposure associations BetaYG_matrix Matrix SNP-outcome associations (rows = SNPs, columns = outcomes/traits) seBetaXG Numeric vector standard errors BetaXG seBetaYG_matrix Matrix standard errors BetaYG_matrix phi Numeric vector bandwidth multipliers sensitivity analysis (default: c(1, 0.5, 0.25)) n_boot Integer, number bootstrap iterations standard error estimation (default: 10000) alpha Numeric, significance level confidence intervals (default: 0.05)

title: Multidimensional Mode-Based Estimation for Mendelian Randomization — IBMODE
text: data frame containing: Method Method name phi Bandwidth multiplier used Estimate.1, Estimate.2, ... Causal effect estimates outcome SE.1, SE.2, ... Standard errors outcome CI_low.1, CI_low.2, ... Lower confidence interval bounds CI_upp.1, CI_upp.2, ... Upper confidence interval bounds P.1, P.2, ... P-values outcome

title: Multidimensional Mode-Based Estimation for Mendelian Randomization — IBMODE
text: function uses weighted multidimensional kernel density estimation find mode joint distribution causal effects across multiple outcomes. Bootstrap resampling used estimate standard errors. Multiple bandwidth parameters (phi) can tested sensitivity analysis.

title: MR-PRESSO with Instrument Borrowing — IBPRESSO
text: Performs Mendelian Randomization Pleiotropy RESidual Sum Outlier (MR-PRESSO) test extended instrument borrowing scenarios. Detects corrects horizontal pleiotropy using auxiliary trait identify outlier instruments.

title: MR-PRESSO with Instrument Borrowing — IBPRESSO
code: IBPRESSO(   BetaOutcome,   BetaExposure,   BetaAux,   SdOutcome,   SdExposure,   SdAux,   data,   OUTLIERtest = FALSE,   DISTORTIONtest = FALSE,   SignifThreshold = 0.05,   NbDistribution = 1000,   seed = NULL,   n_cores = 1 )

title: MR-PRESSO with Instrument Borrowing — IBPRESSO
text: BetaOutcome Character, column name primary outcome SNP effects BetaExposure Character vector, column name(s) exposure SNP effects BetaAux Character, column name auxiliary trait SNP effects (borrowed instrument) SdOutcome Character, column name standard errors outcome effects SdExposure Character vector, column name(s) standard errors exposure effects SdAux Character, column name standard errors auxiliary trait effects data Data frame containing columns OUTLIERtest Logical, whether perform outlier detection (default: FALSE) DISTORTIONtest Logical, whether test outliers cause distortion (default: FALSE) SignifThreshold Numeric, significance threshold tests (default: 0.05) NbDistribution Integer, number null distributions generate (default: 1000) seed Integer, random seed reproducibility (default: NULL) n_cores Integer, number cores parallel processing (default: 1)

title: MR-PRESSO with Instrument Borrowing — IBPRESSO
text: list containing: raw_beta Raw causal effect estimate outlier correction raw_se Standard error raw estimate corrected_beta Outlier-corrected causal effect estimate (outliers detected) corrected_se Standard error corrected estimate p_value Global test p-value horizontal pleiotropy p_distort P-value distortion test (DISTORTIONtest = TRUE) outlier_idx Indices detected outlier instruments n_instruments Number instruments used n_outliers Number outliers detected

title: MR-PRESSO with Instrument Borrowing — IBPRESSO
text: function extends MR-PRESSO handle instrument borrowing incorporating auxiliary trait. uses leave-one-cross-validation Mahalanobis distance bivariate residual space (outcome + auxiliary trait) detect pleiotropic outliers. global test assesses overall horizontal pleiotropy, distortion test evaluates whether removing outliers significantly changes causal estimate.

title: MR-PRESSO with Instrument Borrowing — IBPRESSO
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simulate data set.seed(123) n_snps <- 50 dat <- data.frame(   beta_exposure = rnorm(n_snps, 0.1, 0.02),   beta_outcome = rnorm(n_snps, 0.05, 0.03),   beta_aux = rnorm(n_snps, 0.04, 0.025),   se_exposure = abs(rnorm(n_snps, 0.01, 0.002)),   se_outcome = abs(rnorm(n_snps, 0.015, 0.003)),   se_aux = abs(rnorm(n_snps, 0.012, 0.002)) )  # Run MR-PRESSO with instrument borrowing results <- mrpresso_ib(   BetaOutcome = "beta_outcome",   BetaExposure = "beta_exposure",   BetaAux = "beta_aux",   SdOutcome = "se_outcome",   SdExposure = "se_exposure",   SdAux = "se_aux",   data = dat,   OUTLIERtest = TRUE,   DISTORTIONtest = TRUE,   NbDistribution = 1000 ) } # }

what: Calculate Coheterogeneity Using a Guarded Theoretical Method — coheterogeneity_Q
title: Calculate Coheterogeneity Using a Guarded Theoretical Method — coheterogeneity_Q
text: Computes pairwise coheterogeneity correlations across multiple outcome traits using guarded delta-exact approach. implementation supports optional SNP filtering, weak-instrument filtering, winsorization ratio estimates, LDSC intercept adjustment, per-pair diagnostics.

title: Calculate Coheterogeneity Using a Guarded Theoretical Method — coheterogeneity_Q
code: coheterogeneity_Q(   BetaXG,   BetaYG_matrix = NULL,   seBetaXG,   seBetaYG_matrix = NULL,   BetaIV_matrix = NULL,   seBetaIV_matrix = NULL,   ldsc_intercepts = NULL,   SNP_keep = NULL,   use_ldsc = TRUE,   alpha = 0.05,   step_mult = 0.001,   eps = 1e-12,   bx_min = 1e-06,   F_min = 0,   winsor_theta = 20,   min_K_pair = 50,   return_diagnostics = FALSE )

title: Calculate Coheterogeneity Using a Guarded Theoretical Method — coheterogeneity_Q
text: BetaXG Numeric vector SNP-exposure associations. BetaYG_matrix Matrix SNP-outcome associations, rows = SNPs, columns = traits. Optional BetaIV_matrix provided. seBetaXG Numeric vector standard errors BetaXG. seBetaYG_matrix Matrix standard errors BetaYG_matrix. Optional seBetaIV_matrix provided. BetaIV_matrix Matrix ratio estimates (BetaYG / BetaXG), rows = SNPs, columns = traits. Used reconstruct BetaYG_matrix needed. seBetaIV_matrix Matrix standard errors BetaIV_matrix. Used reconstruct seBetaYG_matrix needed. ldsc_intercepts Optional matrix LDSC intercepts used pairwise covariance adjustment. SNP_keep Optional logical integer index vector specifying SNPs retain. use_ldsc Logical; TRUE, use ldsc_intercepts supplied. alpha Significance level retained interface compatibility. step_mult Scalar used scale finite-difference steps delta method standard error. eps Small positive constant used numerical stabilization. bx_min Minimum absolute exposure effect size allowed. F_min Minimum first-stage F statistic threshold, F = (BetaXG / seBetaXG)^2. winsor_theta Optional cap applied ratio estimates reduce instability small exposure effects. Set NULL disable. min_K_pair Minimum number valid SNPs required trait pair. return_diagnostics Logical; TRUE, include diagnostics list.

title: Calculate Coheterogeneity Using a Guarded Theoretical Method — coheterogeneity_Q
text: list containing: rho Pairwise coheterogeneity correlation matrix. se Pairwise standard error matrix rho. z_statistic Pairwise z-statistic matrix. p_value Pairwise p-value matrix. K Matrix valid SNP counts per trait pair. flag Matrix per-pair diagnostic flags. method Method label estimator. guards List guardrail settings used analysis. diagnostics Optional detailed diagnostics trait pair.

title: Toy Summary-Statistics Example for IBMR — toy_ibmr_example
Toy Summary-Statistics Example for IBMR — toy_ibmr_example
text: named list following entries: BetaXG Numeric vector SNP-exposure associations. seBetaXG Numeric vector standard errors BetaXG. BetaYG_matrix Matrix SNP-outcome associations primary auxiliary traits. seBetaYG_matrix Matrix standard errors matching BetaYG_matrix. primary_trait Name primary outcome column. candidate_auxiliaries Names candidate auxiliary outcome columns. recommended_auxiliary Auxiliary trait intended rank highest toy example. dat_ibpresso_aux1 Data frame formatted IBPRESSO() using recommended auxiliary trait. simulation_setup List describing simulation settings used generate toy data.

title: Toy Summary-Statistics Example for IBMR — toy_ibmr_example
